articles.title: Sensex jumps 600 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,150; investors earn ₹3 lakh crore. What drove the market higher?
articles.summary: Sensex jumps 600 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,150; investors earn ₹3 lakh crore. What drove the market higher?
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Tata Technologies shares jump 9% after expanding strategic partnership with Tenneco
articles.summary: Tata Technologies shares rose 9% to ₹729.20 after announcing an expanded partnership with Tenneco LLC. The agreement involves over $100 million investment to enhance engineering innovation and digital transformation, leveraging India's engineering talent for global automotive needs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Can Nifty 50 end 2026 on a high after a brutal first half? Top sectors and stocks that could deliver solid gains
articles.summary: After facing a near 9% drop in the first half of 2026, hopes for a potential rebound are high. With geopolitical tensions easing, lower oil prices, and a stabilising currency, investors are weighing bets on key sectors like banking, automobiles, and renewable energy for stronger performance ahead.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kajaria Ceramics buyback opens July 3: Check key dates, price, eligibility, and more
articles.summary: Kajaria Ceramics announced a buyback of up to 21.50 lakh equity shares, starting July 3 and closing July 9, 2026. Small shareholders can sell 16 shares for every 127 held, while others can sell 21 shares for every 928. Total buyback is valued at ₹296.70 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Upcoming IPO: FirstCry-backed Swara Baby Products files draft papers to raise over ₹1,000 crore via IPO. Details here
articles.summary: Upcoming IPO: According to the draft red herring prospectus (DRHP), the IPO comprises a fresh issue of equity shares worth up to ₹500 crore and an offer for sale (OFS) of ₹500 crore by existing shareholders.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Room AC firms set to have a moment in the sun?
articles.summary: Room air-conditioner sales surged 40% in Q1FY27 on heatwaves and a delayed monsoon, with Voltas crossing 1 million units in record time. But rising input costs and Bureau of Energy Efficiency-linked price hikes of 5-12% threaten to erode margins even as premium demand stays resilient.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund raises stake to 5.24% in Container Corporation of India
articles.summary: ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund has raised its stake in Container Corporation of India Ltd, acquiring over 44 lakh shares on June 30, 2026. This increased its holdings to 5.24%, surpassing the 5% disclosure threshold mandated by SEBI regulations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Nagaraj Shetti recommends IFCI, Five-Star Business Finance shares to buy in the short-term
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty 50 traded higher as easing crude oil prices boosted investor sentiment. Nifty rose 0.50% to 24,127.15 and Sensex gained 0.48% to 77,294.46 amid positive US-Iran negotiations and a drop in Brent crude prices below $71 per barrel.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Shankar Sharma enters SIP debate, asks critics and supporters to reveal their 'self-interest'
articles.summary: Amid the SIP investment debate, Shankar Sharma stresses the importance of transparency and urges participants to reveal their self-interests. He clarifies that his focus is on broader economic implications, particularly the potential impact on Indian currency and inflation, while supporting SIPs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Can WeWork India's managed-office bet pay off?
articles.summary: WeWork India's stock hit a post-listing high after management struck an upbeat tone on AI-driven demand from global capability centres, but its premium valuation versus peers rests on execution of an expansion plan that carries occupancy and margin risks.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Margin trading: why discount brokers are betting big on MTF despite the risks
articles.summary: After Sebi's crackdown on speculative derivatives trading dented their biggest revenue engine, discount brokers are increasingly relying on margin funding to deepen customer engagement and expand margins. The strategy comes as the regulator seeks to strengthen a rapidly growing leveraged product.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty IT jumps over 4%; Infosys, TCS shares surge up to 5%. Should you buy IT stocks at this juncture?
articles.summary: Amidst a 30% decline in the IT sector this year, the Nifty IT index jumped over 4% as traders speculate if this marks a market recovery. Experts advise caution—monitoring upcoming earnings and AI impacts could determine if now is the time for investments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CSM Technologies share price hits 5% lower circuit after flat listing. Should you buy, sell or hold?
articles.summary: CSM Technologies debuted flat on the Indian stock market, opening at ₹113, matching its issue price. The IPO received a muted response, being subscribed only 1.36 times. The company plans to use the funds raised for growth initiatives and working capital.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Newly listed Waterways Leisure Tourism share price hits 10% upper circuit after weak stock market debut
articles.summary: After a subdued market debut, Waterways Leisure Tourism's shares rallied 10% to a record high of ₹734.05. The company's IPO attracted significant retail interest, achieving 1.46 times subscription, and aims to strengthen its position in India's cruise tourism sector.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Adon Agro Commodities IPO share allotment in focus today: GMP, 4 steps to check status online
articles.summary: Adon Agro Commodities' IPO shares are set to be finalised today. Learn how to check your allotment status online and what the grey market premium indicates about the stock's debut.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger Alert! Vedanta Iron and Steel share price soars 113% since listing - More upside ahead?
articles.summary: After a significant acquisition by Premji Invest, Vedanta Iron & Steel shares surged, reaching an all-time high. Other Vedanta companies also saw gains. Analysts note the stock's upward trend.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CSM Technologies share price makes flat debut, lists at IPO price of ₹113
articles.summary: CSM Technologies share price made a subdued debut on Dalal Street on Thursday. The stock listed at its IPO price of ₹113 on both the BSE and NSE.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying CDSL, Indian Hotels shares on 2 July
articles.summary: Indian stock indices Sensex and Nifty 50 rose on Thursday, boosted by falling crude oil prices after US-Iran negotiations. Nifty 50 gained 0.23%, while Sensex increased by 0.21%. Continued sideways trading seen, with key resistance at 24,100-24,200 and support at 23,850-23,800
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Adani Energy Solutions approves ₹10,000 crore fundraising via QIP; check Q1 results date for the Adani Group stock
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions plans to raise up to ₹10,000 crore via Qualified Institutional Placement, pending shareholder approval. The company will hold an Extraordinary General Meeting on July 25, 2026, to discuss the proposal. Its stock performance shows significant growth over the past year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Wipro share price climbs 1% following a 17% drop in ADRs overnight amid growing AI concerns. Do you own?
articles.summary: Wipro ADRs fell over 16.89% to $1.870 apiece on the NYSE even as ADRs of Infosys rose 1.53% to $10.65, while technology peers Cognizant and Accenture gained 6.04% and 5.38%, respectively.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Tight funding norms for prop traders may hit expiry day volumes
articles.summary: RBI's tighter funding norms make bank overdrafts, widely used by proprietary traders as margins on weekly Nifty and Sensex options expiry days, significantly harder to access.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold price today: MCX gold drops 0.20%, silver climbs 0.50% after soft US jobs data
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices traded mixed in the domestic futures market on Thursday morning, amid largely positive global cues. The dollar index remains above 101, keeping bullion expensive for buyers in overseas currencies.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 32 paise higher at 94.93 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened 32 paise stronger at 94.93 against the US dollar, influenced by falling crude prices. Traders expect limited recovery due to weaknesses in other Asian currencies and rising US Treasury yields, with the RBI likely to manage currency volatility.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Momentum investing stages sharp comeback after 15-month lull
articles.summary: The strategy outpaced value and quality investing styles in the April-June quarter as active capital chased isolated pockets of growth outside major large caps.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Aastha Spintex IPO allotment to be finalised today: Check latest GMP, steps to check allotment status online
articles.summary: Aastha Spintex allotment date is likely to be finalised today, July 2. Investors can check its IPO allotment status online through the websites of NSE, BSE and the IPO registrar - Bigshare Services.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Stocks to buy for short term: NBCC among 3 shares Amol Athawale of Kotak Securities recommends for next 1-2 weeks
articles.summary: Stocks to buy for the short term: Amol Athawale recommends three stocks, including NBCC and CDSL, for the next 1-2 weeks. Discover the investment rationale and target price of each stocks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Knack Packaging IPO Day 2: Issue subscribed 3.45x so far. Check GMP, key dates, review. Apply or not?
articles.summary: Knack Packaging IPO, priced between ₹161-170, opened on July 1 and closes on July 3. It aims to raise ₹380 crore and is showing strong demand with a 2.60x subscription on Day 1, highlighting its market potential.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices extend decline for third session amid ongoing US-Iran talks; Brent crude near $70 per barrel
articles.summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude for September delivery traded near $71 a barrel after falling more than 3% over the previous two sessions.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stocks to watch: Bharti Airtel, Indian Bank, BPCL among shares in focus today; check list here
articles.summary: Stock market today: Gift Nifty was trading near the 24,187.5 mark, down over 95 points from the previous close of Nifty futures.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vaishali Parekh recommends three intraday stocks to buy or sell on Thursday — 2 July 2026
articles.summary: Buy or sell stocks, 2 July 2026: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders, GMDC, and EID Parry
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints flat-to-positive start; eight day trading stocks to buy on Thursday, 2 July
articles.summary: The Indian stock market shows a flat start with Gift Nifty trading at 24,180.5, an 88-point premium. Sensex and Nifty 50 rebounded on 1 July, ending a two-day decline as easing crude oil prices boosted market sentiment amid ongoing US-Iran diplomatic talks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi falls 5%, Japan's Nikkei 225 plunges 2% over AI concerns
articles.summary: Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi plunged 5.36% at the open, prompting the Korea Exchange to impose a five-minute trading halt.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From Gift Nifty to semiconductor stocks, gold rate: 9 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
articles.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,187.5 level, a premium of nearly 95 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a mildly positive start for the Indian stock market indices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 2 July
articles.summary: The trends on Gift Nifty indicate a positive start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 24,187.5 level, a premium of nearly 95 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 2 July
articles.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 2 July. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: 5kg LPG cylinder demand from immigrant labourers plummets up to 80% amid price surge
articles.summary: Demand for these portable gas cylinders creatively named after kids plunged after prices doubled following the West Asia war, at least four distributors said. These 5kg cylinders are primarily used by migrant workers and students, and can be bought off the shelf with just Aadhar as identity proof.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The ‘Hi, hello’ monsoon: In rural India, an anxious, high-stakes wait for the skies to swell
articles.summary: A teasing sky is chocking central India’s fields and threatening harvests worth millions. Some Basmati growers and tribal farmers now face a brutal choice: wait for the rain, or pack for the factory floor.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Manufacturing PMI slows to 54.2 in June on softer demand
articles.summary: It was the second-slowest expansion in four years 2022, better than only March, the first month of West Asia war.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's richest cities: urban affairs ministry is on a quest to assess economic output of cities for first time
articles.summary: The Indian government is creating a standardized methodology to estimate the economic output of cities, known as City Economic Product. The initiative aims to quantify urban economic contributions, aiding in planning and investment, as urban areas contribute nearly 70% of India's economic output.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: GST collections rise 14% to ₹1.95 trillion in June, as tax revenue from imports soars
articles.summary: June GST collections suggest economic activity remained resilient despite global uncertainties, with higher imports and steady domestic transactions supporting tax revenues.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India, Japan eye LNG stockpiling partnership amid global supply uncertainty as Sanae Takaichi arrives today: Report
articles.summary: India and Japan are set to sign an LNG supply deal as PM Sanae Takaichi arrives for a summit with PM Narendra Modi. The agreement aims to address global supply chain disruptions caused by the US-Iran conflict, establishing a joint task force for LNG stockpiling.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Economy holds firm, but risks remain from monsoon and geopolitics, says govt's economic review
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict and deficient monsoon rainfall so far underscore the need to reorient India's agricultural pricing policies to incentivise the cultivation of climate-resilient crops
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's fiscal deficit at 9.6% of FY27 target in May
articles.summary: The government has budgeted a fiscal deficit of 4.3% of GDP, or about ₹16.96 trillion, for FY27 and is expected to rely on stronger tax collections and non-tax receipts in the coming months to keep the deficit within the target.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Morgan Stanley cuts oil price forecast again as Strait of Hormuz flows recover, warns of glut
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley has downgraded its oil forecasts as Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes quicker than expected, coupled with strong US supplies and weak Chinese demand, risking an oil glut. Brent prices are expected to average $75 per barrel for the next two quarters.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The human cost of India’s new welfare architecture, explained in charts
articles.summary: India is shifting from rights-based welfare to new welfarism, prioritizing centralized cash transfers and tangible goods over public services. This transition strains state finances and risks eroding long-term human development.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The women behind India’s economy—and the price of invisible work
articles.summary: A recent Supreme Court judgment places economic value on the work done by homemakers. That value won’t make it into GDP math, but should find its way into policymaking.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Freight rates to stay elevated amid high demand for tankers, vessels
articles.summary: Global shipping freight rates are expected to stay high for the coming months due to increased demand from recovering trade flows and the peak shipping season, despite a slight easing in rates following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 5.1% in May under new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: Growth was led by a robust expansion in manufacturing and supported by moderate growth in mining and electricity, according to provisional data released by the statistics ministry on Monday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India regains top spot in EM tracker, but higher cost pressures continue to linger
articles.summary: Stronger exports and factory activity helped India reclaim the top spot in Mint's Emerging Markets Tracker. However, higher crude prices during the West Asia war have raised wholesale inflation and input costs, delaying relief for businesses despite the recent easing in oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: North-East floods raise power, logistics and inflation risks
articles.summary: The disruptions could have wider consequences for the rest of the country, where power demand remains high amid persistent heat and an uneven monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Modi spotlights shift to public transport, natural fertilizers amid Gulf crisis
articles.summary: Addressing the nation in his monthly radio broadcast ‘Mann Ki Baat’, Modi said that following his appeal to avoid foreign travel and cut gold purchases, many people have postponed overseas holidays and chosen not to buy new gold, with some instead recycling old gold to make new jewellery.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt eases Quality Control Order rules: Which industry segments are likely to get relief?
articles.summary: The government's new transition framework offers relief to manufacturers in sectors such as toys, personal protective equipment (PPE), air conditioners, footwear, furniture and select electrical appliances.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.summary: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Iranian oil may return, but Indian refiners eye concessions
articles.summary: As Indian refiners await clarity in terms of payment mechanism and long-term durability of the US-Iran peace talks, they may not rush for immediate procurement of Iranian crude and are taking a more long-term approach, to ensure supplies when the sanctions are permanently waived off
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Will water shortages derail India’s AI Data Centre boom?
articles.summary: India's ambition to become a leading AI data centre hub brings unprecedented investments, but it also raises crucial questions about sustainability.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Weak core output, Russian crude imports, AI edge
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH
